content.problemStatement:
Neural nets are extremely popular in the Machine Learning domain. A neural net is composed of multiple layers. It has an input layer in which you input the parameter xxx (the input of the program). The input is then passed through multiple hidden layers, finally getting one output at the final layer, called the output layer.

We have a very simple neural net, which consists of NNN hidden layers. Each layer contains one neuron. Each neuron has two values associated with it: wiw_iwi‚Äã, and bib_ibi‚Äã, denoting the weight and the bias of the neuron. If you give the neuron an input of xxx, it produces an output of (wi‚ãÖx)+bi(w_i \cdot x) + b_i(wi‚Äã‚ãÖx)+bi‚Äã.

Thus, an input xxx gets transformed by the neural net as follows. The first hidden neuron takes the input xxx and produces y=w1‚ãÖx+b1y = w_1 \cdot x + b_1y=w1‚Äã‚ãÖx+b1‚Äã, which acts as the input for the second neuron. Then, the second neuron takes input yyy and produces an output of z=w2‚ãÖy+b2z = w_2 \cdot y + b_2z=w2‚Äã‚ãÖy+b2‚Äã. This keeps happening and you get a single output at the end from the NNN-th neuron.

There are some users and we want to find if they are spamming or not. They have integer user-ids, which range from minXminXminX to maxXmaxXmaxX (both inclusive). So we take each of these user-ids and feed it as input to the first layer of the neural net. If the final output is even, then that user is not a spammer, otherwise, the user is a spammer. You have to count the number of non-spammers and spammers.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, minXminXminX, maxXmaxXmaxX.
Each of the next NNN lines contains two space-separated integers wiw_iwi‚Äã and bib_ibi‚Äã denoting the weight and the bias of the iii-th neuron.


Output Format
For each test case, output two space-separated integers denoting the number of non-spammers and the number of spammers, respectively.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example 1. There is a single neuron with weight = 1 and bias = 2. Let us check the output for x=1x = 1x=1: w‚ãÖx+b=1‚ãÖ1+2=3w \cdot x + b = 1 \cdot 1 + 2 = 3w‚ãÖx+b=1‚ãÖ1+2=3. Output for x=2x = 2x=2 would be 2‚ãÖ1+2=42 \cdot 1 + 2 = 42‚ãÖ1+2=4. You can see that one of these is even and the other is odd. So, there is one spammer and one non-spammer.
Example 2. There are two neurons with weights 2 each, but bias 4, 3 respectively.

x=1x = 1x=1, y=2‚ãÖ1+4=6y = 2 \cdot 1 + 4 = 6y=2‚ãÖ1+4=6. z=2‚ãÖ6+3=15z = 2 \cdot 6 + 3 = 15z=2‚ãÖ6+3=15
x=2x = 2x=2, y=2‚ãÖ2+4=8y = 2 \cdot 2 + 4 = 8y=2‚ãÖ2+4=8. z=2‚ãÖ8+3=19z = 2 \cdot 8 + 3 = 19z=2‚ãÖ8+3=19
x=3x = 3x=3, y=2‚ãÖ3+4=10y = 2 \cdot 3 + 4 = 10y=2‚ãÖ3+4=10. z=2‚ãÖ10+3=23z = 2 \cdot 10 + 3 = 23z=2‚ãÖ10+3=23
x=4x = 4x=4, y=2‚ãÖ4+4=12y = 2 \cdot 4 + 4 = 12y=2‚ãÖ4+4=12. z=2‚ãÖ12+3=27z = 2 \cdot 12 + 3 = 27z=2‚ãÖ12+3=27

You can see that all of these are odd and hence signify that they are spammers. So, there are 0 non-spammers and 4 spammers.
